name: Epidermal growth factor receptor
description: EGFR is a transmembrane protein that is a member of the ErbB family of receptors. Mutations and overexpression of EGFR have been implicated in many cancers.

name: Receptor tyrosine-protein kinase erbB-2
description: HER2 is a member of the epidermal growth factor receptor family. Amplification or overexpression of HER2 plays an important role in the development and progression of breast cancer.

name: Serine/threonine-protein kinase B-Raf
description: BRAF is a human gene that makes a protein called B-Raf. The BRAF V600E mutation is found in about 50% of melanomas.

name: Programmed death-ligand 1
description: PD-L1 is a protein that acts as a kind of 'off switch' to keep the immune system from attacking normal cells. Cancer cells can use this to avoid being attacked by the immune system.

name: ALK tyrosine kinase receptor
description: ALK is a receptor tyrosine kinase that plays a role in nervous system development. ALK rearrangements are found in about 5% of non-small cell lung cancers.

name: Vascular endothelial growth factor receptor 2
description: VEGFR2 is a major mediator of VEGF-induced endothelial cell proliferation, survival, migration, and angiogenesis.

name: Tyrosine-protein kinase JAK2
description: JAK2 is a member of the Janus family of tyrosine kinases. The JAK2 V617F mutation is found in most patients with polycythemia vera.

name: Dual specificity mitogen-activated protein kinase kinase 1
description: MEK1 is a kinase that activates ERK1 and ERK2. It is part of the RAS/RAF/MEK/ERK signaling pathway that is frequently mutated in cancer.

name: Phosphatidylinositol 4,5-bisphosphate 3-kinase catalytic subunit alpha
description: PI3K is a family of enzymes involved in cellular functions such as cell growth, proliferation, differentiation and survival.

name: Serine/threonine-protein kinase mTOR
description: mTOR is a serine/threonine kinase that regulates cell growth, cell proliferation, cell motility, cell survival, protein synthesis, autophagy, and transcription.
